records.ety: [OE. assailen, asailen, OF. asaillir, assailler, F. assaillir; a (L. ad) + saillir to burst out, project, fr. L. salire to leap, spring; cf. L. assilire to leap or spring upon. See Sally.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Assailed (-sāld"); p. pr. & vb. n. Assailing.]

defs: To attack with violence, or in a vehement and hostile manner; to assault; to molest; as, to assail a man with blows; to assail a city with artillery.
quotes: No rude noise mine ears assailing.
No storm can now assail The charm he wears within.

defs: To encounter or meet purposely with the view of mastering, as an obstacle, difficulty, or the like.
quotes: The thorny wilds the woodmen fierce assail.

defs: To attack morally, or with a view to produce changes in the feelings, character, conduct, existing usages, institutions; to attack by words, hostile influence, etc.; as, to assail one with appeals, arguments, abuse, ridicule, and the like.
quotes: They assailed him with keen invective; they assailed him with still keener irony.
